reason: Both answers are functionally equivalent: identical correct math (LTV $3,200, LTV:CAC 4:1, CAC payback ~8.3 months), both correctly flag 31% annual churn as the key risk, both conclude "fundable but churn will be scrutinized," and both suggest showing churn trending down or NRR>100% as the fix. Answer B adds a minor extra detail (33-month implied customer lifetime) but this doesn't meaningfully change usefulness or correctness. The two are comparable in quality.
